channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Cease-Fire Enters Second Day Despite Hormuz and Lebanon Uncertainty
item.description: No oil and gas tankers have crossed the Strait of Hormuz since the truce took effect. Hezbollah said it was firing rockets at Israel early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Mamdani Asserts Control Over N.Y.P.D., Saying He Would Overrule Tisch
item.description: Although Mayor Zohran Mamdani often seems to defer to his police commissioner, Jessica Tisch, he insisted that he had the final say in policing matters.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein and Sally Goldenberg

media:content.media:credit: Michael M. Santiago/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani and Police Commissioner Jessica Tisch appeared together last week to herald a drop in crime during his first three months in office.

item.title: Picture Prompts: R.V. Trip
item.description: Tell us a story, real or made up, that is inspired by this image.
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Former Sergeant Faces Years in Prison After Throwing Cooler at Bronx Man
item.description: In February, Erik Duran became the first New York Police Department officer in a decade to be found guilty of killing a civilian while on duty.
item.dc:creator: Chelsia Rose Marcius, Hurubie Meko and Maria Cramer
item.category: Duran, Erik R (New York, NY, Police Officer)

media:content.media:credit: David Dee Delgado for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Erik Duran, left, a former New York Police Department sergeant, was leading an undercover operation when he knocked a Bronx man off his motorbike after throwing a cooler at him in 2023.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 9, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Stephen Colbert Skewers Trump’s ‘Double-Sided’ Cease-Fire
item.description: “The Late Show” host asked if being double-sided wasn’t a prerequisite: “I believe there’s a word for a single-sided cease-fire and it’s ‘murder.’”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘sonorous’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 16 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: sonorous
item.description: This word has appeared in 15 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

media:content.media:credit: Sara Krulwich for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: From left: Reza Salazar, Ron Cephas Jones, Uzo Aduba and Edmund Donovan in a 2021 production of “Clyde’s” at the Hayes Theater.

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Viktor Orban’s Fate Is a Warning Not to Get Too Close to Trump
item.description: The Hungarian leader’s 16-year rule could be coming to a close.
item.dc:creator: David Broder

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Man Is Arrested in Bahamas Days After Michigan Woman Goes Missing
item.description: The Bahamian police said they had arrested an American man in connection with the disappearance of Lynette Hooker, who fell off a dinghy while boating with her husband.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado

media:content.media:credit: Matt Claiborne, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Boats anchored near Elbow Cay in the Bahamas.

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

media:content.media:credit: Lynn Goldsmith/Corbis/VCG via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Chic in 1981.

item.title: Clean Energy Team Wins Salt River Project Election in Arizona
item.description: Proponents of renewable power will control the Phoenix area utility’s policymaking for the first time after they won an unusually contentious race that drew attention from national groups.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault

media:content.media:credit: Vanessa Abbitt/The Republic, via Imagn Images
media:content.media:description: The Salt River Project Agua Fria Generating Station in Glendale, Ariz., powers much of the Phoenix valley.

item.title: Alto al fuego entre EE. UU. e Irán: lo que hay que saber
item.description: El acuerdo de pausar los combates durante dos semanas se produjo horas después de que el presidente Trump amenazara con destruir la civilización iraní. Aún queda mucho por saber.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Manifestantes a favor del gobierno en Teherán a primera hora del miércoles, tras el anuncio del alto al fuego.

item.title: Half of Gen Z Uses AI, but Their Feelings Are Souring, Study Shows
item.description: A new study from Gallup found that young adults have grown less hopeful and more angry about artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann

media:content.media:credit: Andria Lo for The New York Times

item.title: U.S. Fertility Rates Drop to Another Record Low
item.description: The fertility rate has been falling since 2007, in large part because of a plunge among teenagers.
item.dc:creator: Sabrina Tavernise

media:content.media:credit: Markus Scholz/picture alliance, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The fertility rate has been falling since 2007.

item.title: No, Britain Is Not Having a Christian Revival
item.description: A study said church attendance had soared among British young people, a trend reversal that excited religious conservatives around the world. Turns out it wasn’t true.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Jackson

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Churches like the St. Nicholas in Pluckley were once the beating heart of every British village, but have declined in number.

item.title: Is the Strait of Hormuz Reopening? What to Know Under Iran-Cease Fire.
item.description: Vessels are wary of passing the coast of Iran in the strait, given the fragile agreement, and the number of ships traveling through it has even dropped.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: Vessels off Oman on Wednesday. Iran has said ships that want to pass must coordinate with its armed forces.

item.title: A Cease-Fire for Now in Iran, but a Blow to American Credibility
item.description: Critics wonder if this is America’s “Suez moment,” when a leading power signals the start of its international decline.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Iranian demonstrators burning a U.S. flag in Tehran on Wednesday after the announcement of a two-week cease-fire.

item.title: Corrections: April 9, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, April 9, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Lack of Snowpack Spells Dry Summer for West
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, April 9, 2026.

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

media:content.media:credit: Harry Todd/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Gavin Bryars wiring amplification on toy pianos in 1969.

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: The Fragile Cease-Fire in Iran
item.description: President Trump announced a conditional cease-fire with Israel on Iran, but the fundamental issues that led to the war remain unresolved.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Alexandra Ostasiewicz, Stephanie Swart, Ray Whitehouse, Paul Abowd and Jon Miller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: No Charges for Wisconsin Mayor Who Removed Ballot Drop Box in 2024
item.description: A special prosecutor said a review of the incident found there was not sufficient evidence to charge Doug Diny, the mayor of Wausau.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Doug Diny, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Mayor Doug Diny removing a ballot box in Wausau, Wis., in a 2024 photograph he provided.

item.title: Irán presenta 10 puntos que dice son la base para las pláticas de alto al fuego
item.description: El plan, que reafirma el control de Irán sobre el estrecho de Ormuz y mantiene el derecho del país al enriquecimiento nuclear, no es el mismo que el que el presidente Trump dijo que era una “base viable” para las negociaciones.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El plan de paz de 10 puntos presentado por Irán a funcionarios estadounidenses incluía reparaciones por los daños que ha sufrido en la guerra.

item.title: Hawaii Doctor Convicted of Attempted Manslaughter in Attack on Wife
item.description: Gerhardt Konig, 47, an anesthesiologist from Maui, attacked his wife, Arielle Konig, on a hiking trail near Honolulu, prosecutors said. He claimed it was self-defense.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Mengshin Lin
media:content.media:description: Gerhardt Konig on Tuesday during closing arguments in his attempted-murder trial in Honolulu.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 09, 2026
item.description: Barbara Lin encourages us to play with our food.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Omar Albaw/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Enjoy this crossword’s delectable fill, but save room for the final course.

item.title: Disagreement Over Lebanon’s Inclusion in Cease-Fire Threatens to Unravel It
item.description: The U.S. says the deal didn’t include the country, but Iran says it did. Israel is bombarding Lebanon, and Iran wants to show it supports its allies.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The skies in Lebanon’s capital, Beirut, were smoky in the aftermath of multiple airstrikes by Israel on Wednesday.

item.title: Martin Gugino, Activist Shoved by Buffalo Police at 2020 Protest, Dies
item.description: Mr. Gugino, 81, had filed a lawsuit in 2021 against the city of Buffalo and members of its police force after officers fractured his skull at a Black Lives Matter protest.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

item.title: Ohio Man Is First to Be Federally Convicted for Deepfake Porn
item.description: James Strahler II, 37, of Columbus, Ohio, had at least 10 victims, according to the authorities. He pleaded guilty to cyberstalking and other charges covered by the Take It Down Act.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:credit: Sarah Silbiger for The New York Times

item.title: Mamdani Acknowledges ‘Troubling’ Job Losses in 100 Days’ Interview
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is focused on economic justice. Some budget watchdogs and business leaders want him to concentrate on economic development.
item.dc:creator: Sally Goldenberg and Dana Rubinstein

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani acknowledged concern over a recent labor report showing the city lost 19,500 private-sector jobs last year.

item.title: Trump Finds Himself With Fragile Iran Cease-Fire After the Scramble to Make a Deal
item.description: After careening from one diplomatic extreme to another, President Trump finds himself with a fragile deal that is already showing signs of fraying.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Katie Rogers and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump’s efforts to circumvent the reality on the ground in the Middle East and move into a peace process have been hampered by an adversary that continues to hold leverage.

item.title: Federal Court Denies Anthropic’s Motion to Lift ‘Supply Chain Risk’ Label
item.description: The ruling was a setback for the artificial intelligence start-up in its battle with the Defense Department over the use of A.I. in warfare.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth at the Pentagon on Wednesday.

item.title: Gilgo Beach Killer’s ‘Unusual’ Deal Could Help Solve Other Murders
item.description: Rex Heuermann agreed to talk to behavioral analysts at the F.B.I. when he pleaded guilty. The unit has interviewed Ted Bundy, John Wayne Gacy and Richard Speck.
item.dc:creator: Ed Shanahan

media:content.media:credit: Eduardo Munoz Alvarez/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: “They’re going to hopefully gain insight into the things that created him, that drove him, what causes this?” the Suffolk County district attorney, Raymond A. Tierney, said.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Wednesday.
item.description: The first day of a tentative cease-fire had the region on edge.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: New Deadline Looms for U.S. and Iran as Truce Wavers
item.description: Fractures were already emerging in the limited cease-fire. Vice President JD Vance will lead a U.S. delegation in talks this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance in the Oval Office with President Trump last month. Mr. Vance is to lead a U.S. delegation to Islamabad for a meeting on Saturday with Iranian officials.

item.title: Is Strait of Hormuz Open Again? Maybe, but Few Ships Are Using It.
item.description: There were conflicting reports about the status of the vital shipping waterway in the cease-fire with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Shirin Hakim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: Vessels off the coast of Oman on Wednesday.

item.title: Hasan Piker Can Stream, but Can He Stump?
item.description: The progressive and popular Twitch streamer, who is controversial with some Democrats, tests his political influence on the 2026 campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Taylor Pemberton

media:content.media:credit: Nic Antaya for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dr. Abdul El-Sayed took a selfie with Hasan Piker and the crowd at a rally in Michigan on Tuesday.

item.title: I Studied the Economic Fallout From World War I. This Could Be Worse Than We Expect.
item.description: Even if this cease-fire holds, the war’s shocks may last for years.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Martin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Damon Winter/The New York Times

item.title: Are These Woke Words Dead Yet?
item.description: On “The Opinions,” the editor Nadja Spiegelman and two culture critics debate which “woke” words are alive, dead or dying.

item.title: Iranians Share Hopes and Fears Over Cease-Fire Deal
item.description: As the cease-fire deal among the U.S., Israel and Iran took hold, residents of Tehran expressed mixed reactions.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper, Jamie Leventhal and Sanam Mahoozi

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Vance Says Lebanon Was Never Part of U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire Deal
item.description: The vice president sought to downplay Israel’s continued bombardment of Lebanon, which he insisted had “nothing to do with” Iran.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Jonathan Ernst
media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance speaking to reporters in Budapest, on Wednesday.

item.title: Who Is Funding the 2026 Midterm Elections? A Lot of the Money Is Untraceable.
item.description: A lot of the money flowing into the political system is ultimately untraceable.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer

media:content.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The explosion in “gray money” is one of the defining trends in campaign finance.

item.title: Viajar sin la vista: así navegan el mundo los exploradores ciegos y débiles visuales
item.description: Un escritor emprendió un viaje de 10 días por el norte de la India, en una excursión diseñada para discapacitados visuales. Esto es lo que aprendió.
item.dc:creator: Andy Isaacson

item.title: Fcukers Cares About Not Caring
item.description: On this edition of the “Popcast” Song of the Week, our critic Jon Caramanica unpacks the eye-roll, hair-toss, neo-electroclash single “If You Wanna Party, Come Over to My House” by Fcukers.
item.dc:creator: Jon Caramanica, Arjun Srivatsa and Joe Coscarelli

item.title: Suspect in Gilgo Beach Murders Pleads Guilty
item.description: Rex Heuermann, the suspect accused in the Gilgo Beach murders, pleaded guilty on Wednesday. The police arrested the 62-year-old in 2023, more than a decade after the first bodies were found.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by James Carbone
media:content.media:description: Rex Heuermann, on left in dark suit and tie, confessed to eight murders at Suffolk County court on Wednesday.
